target.name: Mainspring Energy

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $650M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $15.5B

data.narrative:
Mainspring Energy is a Series F climate & energy company that has raised $650M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises nine same-sector peers—one public (Lnza) and eight private—including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, narrow peer comparability is constrained; applying same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier filters yields insufficient overlap (fewer than three datapoints) to support a robust peer-median approach.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. Using total capital raised ($650M) as a valuation proxy, a standard Series F-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.3x yields an implied valuation of $845M. This method reflects typical capital efficiency expectations at Mainspring's maturity level.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $718M (low) to $972M (high), with a median estimate of $845M. This range incorporates ±15% sensitivity bounds around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly comparable same-stage transactions (limiting external validation), technology de-risking progress relative to peer timelines, and customer adoption velocity in the thermal energy storage segment. A broader peer set or recent transaction data in frontier-energy infrastructure would materially strengthen conviction around this range.
